**Step 1: Prioritize Explicit Diagnostic Coding for Lipid Abnormalities (Primary Evidence)**
I have thoroughly reviewed the [Current Visit Record] under "Diagnoses made." The diagnoses listed are: Pneumonitis due to inhalation of food or vomitus; Tracheoesophageal fistula, esophageal atresia and stenosis; Acute kidney failure, unspecified; Acute pancreatitis; Other colostomy and enterostomy complication; Fistula of intestine, excluding rectum and anus; Unspecified protein-calorie malnutrition; Obstructive chronic bronchitis with (acute) exacerbation; Hyposmolality and/or hyponatremia; Chronic cholecystitis; Enlargement of lymph nodes; Postsurgical hypothyroidism; Dyskinesia of esophagus; Unspecified essential hypertension; Paralysis agitans; Depressive disorder, not elsewhere classified; Anxiety state, unspecified; Personal history of malignant neoplasm of thyroid.
Crucially, **there is no explicit diagnosis coded** for hypercholesterolemia, hyperlipidemia, hyperlipoproteinemia, or any other form of lipid disorder within the current record
